title: csv.received
description: Published by dis-ui-server once a tenant's CSV upload is confirmed saved in GCS (the signed-PUT object has finalized). Consumed by the csv-ingest-worker to begin Phase 2 ingest (preflight, PII gate, bronze write, ingress.ready publish). This is the trigger boundary between the UI-facing Phase 1 (dis-ui-server upload-session endpoint, which mints trace_id and issues the signed URL) and the asynchronous Phase 2 worker. Distinct from ingress.ready: csv.received precedes the bronze write and carries no bronze_ref; ingress.ready is published by the worker after bronze lands.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Incremented only on breaking changes.

trace_id.description: Unique identifier for this ingress, minted by dis-ui-server's upload-session endpoint at Phase 1 (hard rule 4: trace_id is generated upstream and never minted mid-pipeline). The worker reads this value and propagates it; it does not mint a new one.

tenant_id.description: Internal tenant UUID, identical to Customer Master core.tenants.id and identity_mirror.tenants.tenant_id. Load-bearing identity: the worker writes bronze and sets RLS context with this. Used as the Pub/Sub ordering key.

store_id.description: Internal store UUID, identical to Customer Master core.stores.id and identity_mirror.stores.store_id. Load-bearing identity. An upload session is bound to exactly one store at session creation.

tenant_display_code.description: Customer Master's authoritative external code for the tenant (tenants.display_code, e.g. 'buc-ees'). Optional in the schema, but producers MUST populate it when publishing; it exists for human and ops readability, never as a substitute for tenant_id. Not the load-bearing identity.

store_code.description: Customer Master's authoritative external code for the store (stores.store_code). Optional in the schema, but producers MUST populate it when publishing (when present in Customer Master; the source column is nullable). Readability only, never a substitute for store_id.

source_id.description: Source registration identifier (POS type, ERP type, etc.) for the upload.

upload_session_id.description: The upload session this CSV was uploaded under, created by dis-ui-server at Phase 1. The worker does NOT resolve identity from this (identity is carried on this message and trusted, since dis-ui-server resolved it at Phase 1). Its role is the source_payload_id component of the idempotency key and lineage back to the business upload event. Distinct from trace_id: trace_id traces this pipeline run, upload_session_id traces the tenant's upload action.

gcs_uri.description: Full GCS URI of the saved CSV payload. Path scheme enforced by libs/dis-storage; the tenant segment is the internal tenant UUID (not an external code), because the UUID is immutable while external codes are user-editable.

received_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the CSV save in GCS was confirmed and this event was published.
